channel.title: Spanner on MCP Toolbox for Databases
channel.description: Recent content in Spanner on MCP Toolbox for Databases

item.title: spanner-execute-sql
item.description: About A spanner-execute-sql tool executes a SQL statement against a Spanner database. It’s compatible with any of the following sources: spanner spanner-execute-sql takes one input parameter sql and run the sql statement against the source. Note: This tool is intended for developer assistant workflows with human-in-the-loop and shouldn’t be used for production agents. Example tools: execute_sql_tool: kind: spanner-execute-sql source: my-spanner-instance description: Use this tool to execute sql statement. Reference field type required description kind string true Must be “spanner-execute-sql”.

item.title: spanner-sql
item.description: About A spanner-sql tool executes a pre-defined SQL statement (either googlesql or postgresql) against a Cloud Spanner database. It’s compatible with any of the following sources: spanner GoogleSQL For the googlesql dialect, the specified SQL statement is executed as a data manipulation language (DML) statements, and specified parameters will inserted according to their name: e.g. @name. Note: This tool uses parameterized queries to prevent SQL injections. Query parameters can be used as substitutes for arbitrary expressions.
